---
title: "6400 Data Proc"
author: "Jackson Strand"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r library}
library(readxl)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)
```

```{r}
clean_licor_with_samples <- function(file) {
  raw <- read_excel(file, col_names = FALSE)
  
  # Find header row
  header_row <- which(raw[[1]] == "Obs")[1]
  
  # Read with header
  dat <- read_excel(file, skip = header_row - 1)
  
  # Remove units row (the "in"/"out" row)
  dat <- dat[-1, ]
  
  colnames(dat)[1] <- "Obs"
  colnames(dat)[2] <- "HHMMSS"
  
  # Extract rep and wk from filename
  base <- basename(file)
  rep_val <- str_extract(base, "(?<=tf[-_])[a-zA-Z]+(?=[-_]wk)")
  wk_val  <- str_extract(base, "wk\\d+")
  
  dat <- dat %>%
    mutate(
      temp_sample_id = ifelse(
        grepl("^Remark=", Obs),
        str_extract(HHMMSS, "[dy]\\d+[ic]"),
        NA_character_
      )
    ) %>%
    fill(temp_sample_id, .direction = "down") %>%
    filter(!grepl("^Remark=", Obs) | grepl("[dy]\\d+[ic]", HHMMSS)) %>%
    rename(sample_id = temp_sample_id) %>%
    mutate(
      across(-c(Obs, HHMMSS, sample_id), as.numeric),
      rep = rep_val,
      wk  = wk_val,
      species   = str_extract(sample_id, "^[a-zA-Z]"),
      plant     = str_extract(sample_id, "\\d+"),
      treatment = case_when(
        str_extract(sample_id, "[a-zA-Z]$") == "c" ~ "control",
        str_extract(sample_id, "[a-zA-Z]$") == "i" ~ "infested",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
)
  
  return(dat)
}
```

```{R run function}
# Apply to all files
files <- list.files("Data_XLSX", pattern = "\\.xlsx$", full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
data_list <- lapply(files, clean_licor_with_samples)
all_data <- bind_rows(data_list)

names(all_data)

all_data %>%
  summarise(across(c(sample_id, species, plant, treatment), ~sum(is.na(.))))
```

```{r changes to data}
# forgot to put in remark for a few data collection points
all_data <- all_data %>%
  mutate(sample_id = case_when(
    rep == "b" & wk == "wk4" & Obs == "1" ~ "d6i",
    rep == "b" & wk == "wk4" & Obs == "2" ~ "d5i",
    rep == "b" & wk == "wk4" & Obs == "3" ~ "d4i",
    rep == "b" & wk == "wk4" & Obs == "4" ~ "d3c",
    rep == "b" & wk == "wk4" & Obs == "5" ~ "d1i",
    rep == "b" & wk == "wk4" & Obs == "6" ~ "d2i",
    TRUE ~ sample_id
  )) %>% 
  # recompute the species, plant, treatment columns
  mutate(
  species   = str_extract(sample_id, "^[a-zA-Z]"),
  plant     = str_extract(sample_id, "\\d+"),
  treatment = case_when(
    str_extract(sample_id, "[a-zA-Z]$") == "c" ~ "control",
    str_extract(sample_id, "[a-zA-Z]$") == "i" ~ "infested",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
) %>% 
  mutate(sample_id = case_when(
    rep == "a" & wk == "wk1" & Obs == "2" ~ "d1i",
    TRUE ~ sample_id
  )) %>% 
  na.omit()

all_data_heights <-
read_excel("stem_heights.xlsx") %>%
  mutate(
    wk = paste0("wk", week),
    rep = as.character(rep)
  ) %>%
  select(sample_id, rep, wk, height) %>% 
  left_join(all_data, by = c("sample_id", "rep", "wk"))



```


```{r height model}
# ‚îÄ‚îÄ Option A: Height as response variable ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
# Collapse to one row per plant per week first
height_dat <- all_data_heights %>%
  filter(rep != "a") %>%                        # consistent with your clean dataset
  group_by(sample_id, rep, wk, species, treatment, plant) %>%
  summarise(height = mean(height, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("control", "infested")),
    species   = factor(species),
    wk        = factor(wk, levels = c("wk1", "wk2", "wk4")),
    rep       = factor(rep)
  )

# Check n per cell
height_dat %>% count(treatment, species, wk)

# Fit model
model_height <- lm(
  height ~ treatment * species * wk + rep,
  data = height_dat
)

Anova(model_height, type = "III")

# emmeans contrasts
emm_height_species <- emmeans(model_height, pairwise ~ treatment | species, adjust = "tukey")
emm_height_week    <- emmeans(model_height, pairwise ~ treatment | wk, adjust = "tukey")

summary(emm_height_species$contrasts)
summary(emm_height_week$contrasts)

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ Option B: Height as covariate in gas exchange models ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
model_photo_height <- lm(
  Photo ~ treatment * species + treatment * wk + rep + height,
  data = filter(all_data_heights, rep != "a")
)

Anova(model_photo_height, type = "III")

# Get the interaction contrasts properly ‚Äî treatment effect at each week x species combo
emm_height_full <- emmeans(model_height, 
                            pairwise ~ treatment | species + wk, 
                            adjust = "tukey")
summary(emm_height_full$contrasts)

```

```{r stem height plot}
dev.off()
# Trajectory plot for stem height ‚Äî mirroring the gas exchange plot structure
ggplot(all_data_heights %>% 
         mutate(wk = factor(wk, 
                            levels = paste0("wk", 
                                sort(unique(as.numeric(str_extract(wk, "\\d+"))))))), 
       aes(x = wk, y = height, color = treatment, group = treatment)) +
  # raw points jittered behind
  geom_jitter(aes(group = treatment), alpha = 0.15, size = 1.2,
              position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.1, dodge.width = 0.3)) +
  # mean trajectory line
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line",
               aes(group = treatment), linewidth = 1, position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  # mean point
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point",
               size = 3, position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  # SE error bars
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar",
               width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.7, position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  facet_grid(rep ~ species, 
             labeller = labeller(
               species = c(d = "Dalmatian Toadflax", y = "Yellow Toadflax")
             )) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("control" = "#2196F3", "infested" = "#E53935"),
    labels = c("Control", "Infested")
  ) +
  labs(
    title   = "Stem Height Trajectories Over Time",
    subtitle = "Mean ¬± SE; points show individual plants",
    x       = "Week",
    y       = "Stem Height (cm)",
    color   = "Treatment"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey92"),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position  = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )
```



```{R look for NAs}
all_data %>%
  filter(is.na(sample_id)) %>%
  select(Obs, HHMMSS, rep, wk) %>%
  print(n = 23)

all_data %>%
  filter(is.na(sample_id)) %>%
  select(Obs, rep, wk, Photo, Cond) %>%
  print(n = 22)

all_data %>%
  group_by(rep, wk) %>%
  mutate(next_sample = lead(sample_id)) %>%
  filter(is.na(sample_id)) %>%
  select(Obs, HHMMSS, rep, wk, next_sample) %>%
  print(n = 23)
```


```{r plot prep}

plot_dat <- all_data %>%
  # Make week ordered so it plots in the right order
  mutate(
    wk = factor(wk, levels = paste0("wk", sort(unique(as.numeric(str_extract(wk, "\\d+")))))),
    treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("control", "infested")),
    species = factor(species)
  ) %>%
  # Pivot the variables you care about into long form
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Photo, Cond, Trmmol, Ci),   # adjust names to match your actual column names
    names_to  = "variable",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(variable = recode(variable,
    Photo  = "Photosynthesis (¬µmol/m¬≤/s)",
    Cond   = "Conductance (mol/m¬≤/s)",
    Trmmol = "Transpiration (mmol/m¬≤/s)",
    Ci     = "Ci (¬µmol/mol)"
  ))

```




```{r plot}
ggplot(plot_dat, aes(x = wk, y = value, color = treatment, group = treatment)) +
  # raw points, jittered lightly
  geom_jitter(aes(group = treatment), alpha = 0.15, size = 1.2,
              position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.1, dodge.width = 0.3)) +
  # mean trajectory line
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line",
               aes(group = treatment), linewidth = 1, position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  # mean point on top
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point",
               size = 3, position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  # SE error bars
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar",
               width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.7, position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  facet_grid(variable ~ species, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("control" = "#2196F3", "infested" = "#E53935"),
    labels = c("Control", "Infested")
  ) +
  labs(
    title   = "Gas Exchange Trajectories Over Time",
    subtitle = "Mean ¬± SE; points show individual observations",
    x       = "Week",
    y       = NULL,
    color   = "Treatment"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    strip.background   = element_rect(fill = "grey92"),
    strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold"),
    strip.text.y       = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0),  # readable y-strip labels
    legend.position    = "top",
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )
```




```{r model}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)   # gives p-values via Satterthwaite approximation
library(broom.mixed)
library(dplyr)

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ 1. Prep: ensure correct types ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
model_dat <- all_data %>%
  mutate(
    treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("control", "infested")),  # control = reference
    species   = factor(species),
    wk        = factor(wk, levels = paste0("wk", sort(unique(as.numeric(str_extract(wk, "\\d+")))))),
    plant     = factor(plant),
    rep       = factor(rep)
  )

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ 2. Fit one mixed model per response variable ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
response_vars <- c("Photo", "Cond", "Trmmol", "Ci")

models <- lapply(setNames(response_vars, response_vars), function(rv) {
  formula <- as.formula(paste(
    rv, "~ treatment * species + treatment * wk + (1 | rep/plant)"
  ))
  lmer(formula, data = model_dat, REML = TRUE)
})

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ 3. ANOVA-style significance table for each model ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
anova_tables <- lapply(models, anova)  # Type III F-tests via lmerTest
anova_tables

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ 4. Tidy coefficient summaries ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
coef_tables <- lapply(models, function(m) tidy(m, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE))

# Print each cleanly
for (rv in response_vars) {
  cat("\n‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê\n")
  cat("Response:", rv, "\n")
  cat("‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê\n")
  print(anova_tables[[rv]])
}

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ 5. Check random effects variance (are rep/plant explaining much?) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
for (rv in response_vars) {
  cat("\nVariance components ‚Äî", rv, "\n")
  print(VarCorr(models[[rv]]))
}

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ 6. Post-hoc contrasts (treatment effect within each species & week) ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
library(emmeans)

# Treatment effect by species
emm_species <- lapply(models, function(m) {
  emmeans(m, pairwise ~ treatment | species, adjust = "tukey")
})

# Treatment effect by week
emm_week <- lapply(models, function(m) {
  emmeans(m, pairwise ~ treatment | wk, adjust = "tukey")
})

# Print contrasts for Photo as example (repeat for others)
summary(emm_species$Photo$contrasts)
summary(emm_week$Photo$contrasts)

# ‚îÄ‚îÄ 7. Quick assumption checks ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
# Run for each model ‚Äî look for roughly normal, homogeneous residuals
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
for (rv in response_vars) {
  plot(models[[rv]], main = rv)           # residuals vs fitted
  qqnorm(resid(models[[rv]]), main = rv)  # Q-Q plot
  qqline(resid(models[[rv]]))
}
```




```{r check a few things}
# 1. How many observations per cell? Small n inflates SE dramatically
model_dat %>%
  count(treatment, species, wk)

# 2. Look at the within-cell variance ‚Äî are there outliers driving wide SE?
model_dat %>%
  group_by(treatment, species, wk) %>%
  summarise(mean = mean(Photo, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd   = sd(Photo, na.rm = TRUE),
            n    = n(), .groups = "drop")

# 3. Run the same contrasts for your other response variables
# Sometimes Cond and Trmmol are more sensitive to infestation than Photo
summary(emm_week$Cond$contrasts)
summary(emm_week$Trmmol$contrasts)
summary(emm_week$Ci$contrasts)

summary(emm_species$Cond$contrasts)
summary(emm_species$Trmmol$contrasts)
summary(emm_species$Ci$contrasts)
```


```{r look for issues}
# 1. Visualize the distribution ‚Äî find the problematic observations
model_dat %>%
  filter(species == "d", treatment == "control") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = wk, y = Photo, label = paste(rep, plant, sep = "-"))) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = rep), width = 0.1, size = 2) +
  geom_text(position = position_jitter(width = 0.1), size = 3, vjust = -0.5) +
  labs(title = "Control species d ‚Äî Photo by week", subtitle = "Looking for outliers")

# 2. Flag statistically extreme values (>3 SD from group mean)
model_dat %>%
  group_by(treatment, species, wk) %>%
  mutate(
    group_mean = mean(Photo, na.rm = TRUE),
    group_sd   = sd(Photo, na.rm = TRUE),
    z_score    = (Photo - group_mean) / group_sd
  ) %>%
  filter(abs(z_score) > 3) %>%
  select(rep, plant, wk, treatment, species, Photo, z_score)

# 3. Also check overall range ‚Äî are there biologically impossible values?
model_dat %>%
  summarise(
    min_Photo = min(Photo, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_Photo = max(Photo, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_negative = sum(Photo < 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# 1. Find and inspect the negative values
model_dat %>%
  filter(Photo < 0) %>%
  select(rep, plant, wk, treatment, species, Photo, Cond, Trmmol, Ci)

# 2. While you're at it, check the other variables for impossible values
model_dat %>%
  summarise(
    across(c(Photo, Cond, Trmmol, Ci), 
           list(min = ~min(., na.rm = TRUE), 
                max = ~max(., na.rm = TRUE),
                n_neg = ~sum(. < 0, na.rm = TRUE)))
  )
```

```{r}
# Remove the clearly bad session (rep c, wk1, species d)
# and the borderline negative separately so you can be transparent about it
model_dat_clean <- model_dat %>%
  filter(
    !(rep == "c" & wk == "wk1" & species == "d"),  # whole bad session
    Photo >= 0                                        # catches the rep b outlier too
  )

# Confirm what you lost
cat("Rows removed:", nrow(model_dat) - nrow(model_dat_clean))

model_dat_clean %>%
  count(treatment, species, wk) %>%
  print(n = Inf)

# Refit all models on clean data
models_clean <- lapply(setNames(response_vars, response_vars), function(rv) {
  formula <- as.formula(paste(
    rv, "~ treatment * species + treatment * wk + (1 | rep/plant)"
  ))
  lmer(formula, data = model_dat_clean, REML = TRUE)
})

# Rerun emmeans contrasts
emm_species_clean <- lapply(models_clean, function(m) {
  emmeans(m, pairwise ~ treatment | species, adjust = "tukey")
})

emm_week_clean <- lapply(models_clean, function(m) {
  emmeans(m, pairwise ~ treatment | wk, adjust = "tukey")
})

# Check Photo first to see if cleaning helped
summary(emm_species_clean$Photo$contrasts)
summary(emm_week_clean$Photo$contrasts)
```

```{r}
# Check which random effect is the problem
VarCorr(models_clean$Photo)

# Almost certainly rep has ~0 variance, so test a simpler structure
model_plant_only <- lmer(
  Photo ~ treatment * species + treatment * wk + (1 | plant),
  data = model_dat_clean, REML = TRUE
)

model_rep_fixed <- lmer(
  Photo ~ treatment * species + treatment * wk + rep + (1 | plant),
  data = model_dat_clean, REML = TRUE
)

# Compare ‚Äî if rep variance was ~0, these will give similar results
VarCorr(model_plant_only)
anova(model_plant_only, model_rep_fixed)  # is rep worth keeping at all?
```

```{r power check}
library(simr)

# What power do you have to detect the treatment effect you observed?
powerSim(models_clean$Photo, 
         test = fixed("treatmentinfested", "t"),
         nsim = 200)

# What sample size would give you 80% power?
# Extend the number of plants and simulate
models_clean$Photo %>%
  extend(along = "plant", n = 30) %>%
  powerCurve(test = fixed("treatmentinfested", "t"), 
             along = "plant", nsim = 100)
```

```{r correct model}
# Best structure given your data: rep fixed, no random effects needed
model_final <- lm(
  Photo ~ treatment * species + treatment * wk + rep,
  data = model_dat_clean
)

summary(model_final)
anova(model_final)  # Type I ‚Äî use car::Anova for Type III

library(car)
Anova(model_final, type = "III")

# Rerun emmeans with this model
emm_species_final <- emmeans(model_final, pairwise ~ treatment | species, adjust = "tukey")
emm_week_final    <- emmeans(model_final, pairwise ~ treatment | wk, adjust = "tukey")

summary(emm_species_final$contrasts)
summary(emm_week_final$contrasts)

# Fit all four response variables with same structure
models_final <- lapply(setNames(response_vars, response_vars), function(rv) {
  formula <- as.formula(paste(rv, "~ treatment * species + treatment * wk + rep"))
  lm(formula, data = model_dat_clean)
})

# ANOVA tables for all
lapply(models_final, function(m) Anova(m, type = "III"))

```


```{r}
# Close any open graphics devices
dev.off()

# Plot one at a time ‚Äî hit Enter in the console to advance each plot
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
plot(models_final$Photo, which = 1)  # Residuals vs Fitted
plot(models_final$Photo, which = 2)  # Q-Q plot
plot(models_final$Photo, which = 3)  # Scale-Location
plot(models_final$Photo, which = 5)  # Residuals vs Leverage
```


```{r}
# Remove rep a and refit
model_dat_norep_a <- model_dat_clean %>%
  filter(rep != "a")

# Check cell counts after removal
model_dat_norep_a %>%
  count(treatment, species, wk) %>%
  print(n = Inf)

# Refit all models
models_norep_a <- lapply(setNames(response_vars, response_vars), function(rv) {
  formula <- as.formula(paste(rv, "~ treatment * species + treatment * wk + rep"))
  lm(formula, data = model_dat_norep_a)
})

# Type III ANOVA tables for all
lapply(models_norep_a, function(m) Anova(m, type = "III"))

# Rerun emmeans contrasts
emm_species_nora <- lapply(models_norep_a, function(m) {
  emmeans(m, pairwise ~ treatment | species, adjust = "tukey")
})

emm_week_nora <- lapply(models_norep_a, function(m) {
  emmeans(m, pairwise ~ treatment | wk, adjust = "tukey")
})

# Print contrasts for all variables
for (rv in response_vars) {
  cat("\n‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê\n")
  cat("Response:", rv, "\n")
  cat("‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê‚ïê\n")
  cat("By species:\n")
  print(summary(emm_species_nora[[rv]]$contrasts))
  cat("By week:\n")
  print(summary(emm_week_nora[[rv]]$contrasts))
}
```
